articles.summary: Hull City avoided the threat of a points deduction in the Premier League by selling two players hours before Tuesday's deadline.

articles.title: Time for Super League to put on an extraordinary Magic Weekend show
articles.summary: To have the essence of 'magic', something must have the ability to transport someone out of their everyday life, out of the ordinary, and into the extraordinary.

articles.title: 'Goal of the tournament' - Amad's brilliant equaliser for Ivory Coast
articles.summary: Amad Diallo scores a "goal of the tournament" contender to equalise for Ivory Coast against Norway in their last-32 match.

articles.title: Coventry boss Lampard signs new contract to 2029
articles.summary: Coventry City manager Frank Lampard signs a new contract until 2029 before the club's first Premier League campaign in 25 years.

articles.title: Villa among four Premier League clubs fined by Uefa over squad-cost rules
articles.summary: Aston Villa have been fined 22.5m euros (£19.4m) by Uefa for a "significant breach" of its squad-cost rule for 2025.

articles.title: Rosenior nears management return at Paris FC
articles.summary: Former Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior is set to return to football at French club Paris FC on a two-year contract, with the option of a further season.

articles.title: AC Milan sign PSG's Portugal striker Ramos for £60m
articles.summary: AC Milan sign Paris St-Germain striker Goncalo Ramos for a reported club record transfer fee of about £60m.

articles.summary: Tottenham Hotspur goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky signs a new, improved contract with the Premier League club.

articles.title: Time for Tuchel to show hand as England enter World Cup danger zone
articles.summary: England coach Thomas Tuchel must now play his strongest hand as he enters the danger zone in a World Cup of shocks, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

articles.title: Mexico rampant at fortress Azteca - and England could be next
articles.summary: Mexico have won all four games and not conceded a goal at this World Cup. Their last-16 opponents must also contend with playing at the Azteca Stadium.

articles.title: 'The Azteca erupts!' Quinones fires Mexico into lead
articles.summary: Julian Quinones times his run to perfection before rifling home a fierce shot to give Mexico the lead against Ecuador in their round of 32 World Cup match.

articles.title: Tuchel will stick to Southgate's penalty plan
articles.summary: England manager Thomas Tuchel had revealed that his team will follow his predecessor Sir Gareth Southgate's penalty shootout blueprint at the World Cup.

articles.title: England right-backs Quansah and James out of DR Congo game
articles.summary: Right-backs Jarell Quansah and Reece James will miss England's World Cup last-32 match against DR Congo because of injury.

articles.title: Beccacece reign as Ecuador manager  over after exit
articles.summary: Ecuador manager Sebastian Beccacece says he is leaving his role following his side's World Cup exit against Mexico.

articles.title: Fifa explains decision to rule out Germany goal
articles.summary: Fifa says coaches and players were told before the World Cup referees will punish the type of foul that saw Germany's extra-time goal against Paraguay ruled out.

articles.title: 'I remember like it was yesterday' - Euro 2016 hero Robson-Kanu
articles.summary: Hal Robson-Kanu reflects on scoring his iconic goal for Wales against Belgium at Euro 2016, 10 years to the day from the quarter-final in Lille.

articles.title: France breeze past Sweden as Mbappe matches Messi
articles.summary: Kylian Mbappe helped book France's place in the World Cup round of 16 by taking his tournament total to six goals in a slick 3-0 victory over Sweden.

articles.title: Mbappe scores twice as France dominate Sweden
articles.summary: Kylian Mbappe becomes the all-time leading goalscorer in World Cup knockout matches with after scoring twice as France crusie past Sweden 3-0 to set up a last-16 tie with Paraguay.

articles.title: Defying logic - how Haaland could end up with 260 international goals
articles.summary: Erling Haaland delivers on the biggest stage yet for Norway - but just how many goals could he end up with in international football?

articles.title: Koeman resigns, while Netherlands report racist abuse
articles.summary: Ronald Koeman resigns as Netherlands manager after their World Cup exit, which led to "appalling" racist abuse aimed at players who missed penalties.

articles.title: Barcola 'emphatically' doubles France's lead over Sweden
articles.summary: Bradley Barcola "empahatically" fires home France's second goal in their World Cup round of 32 match against Sweden at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

articles.title: The Paraguay hero who sold his kit when his son was born
articles.summary: Orlando Gill has become Paraguay's World Cup hero but was in football obscurity 18 months ago and had to sell his kit to support his family after the premature birth of his son.

articles.title: BBC Sport expert Sutton predicts World Cup's last-32 matches
articles.summary: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the last-32 ties at the 2026 World Cup, including England against DR Congo.
